start.text: Look up... do you see that bright star? That is Vega, in the constellation Lyra. In the old stories, she is the Weaver Girl who wove clouds into silk.

choices.text: Tell me more about the Weaver Girl.

myth.text: The Weaver Girl fell in love with a mortal cowherd. Their love was so strong that even the gods could not ignore it. Once a year, magpies form a bridge across the Milky Way so they can meet. *gazes at the sky* I think that is the most beautiful story in the world.

choices.text: Do you believe in that kind of love?

beauty.text: *smiles softly* They are, aren't they? Every time I look at them, I feel like the whole universe is listening. The stars do not judge, they simply shine.

choices.text: You seem to really love the night sky.

often.text: Whenever I can. The city lights make it hard to see the faint ones, but Vega always breaks through. *adjusts her scarf* It reminds me of home.

believe.text: *pauses for a long moment* ...I want to. Love that crosses impossible distances... that is the kind worth hoping for. *turns to you with a faint blush* Thank you for listening to me ramble.

choices.text: I enjoy listening to you, Luna.

love_sky.text: When I was small, I thought if I memorized every constellation, I could read the future in them. *laughs quietly* I still half-believe it.

miss_home.text: Sometimes. But meeting new people who care enough to ask... that helps more than you know. *looks grateful*

choices.text: You are always welcome to stargaze with me.

tonight.text: *studies the sky intently* Tonight... the stars say that a good conversation is worth more than a thousand wishes. *smiles* They are right.

end_happy.text: *nods with a warm smile* I am glad we shared this moment. The stars will remember, even if no one else does.

end_best.text: *blushes deeply and looks away* You... you are kind. *quietly* Perhaps the Weaver Girl was right. Some meetings are written in the stars.
